summary: A devastating drone strike on a college in Starobelsk raised the death toll to 21, while Ukraine’s forces hit key Russian logistics targets including the Sheshharis oil terminal and a shadow‑fleet tanker. Russia continued illicit grain shipments, with the vessel ZAID transporting over 35,000 t of stolen Ukrainian wheat to Turkey. Intense missile and drone activity persisted across multiple fronts, highlighted by a massive combined rocket barrage on Kyiv causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.
key_points: Drone strike on Starobelsk college killed 21, search operations concluded (Source: @tass_agency/377250).
Ukrainian drones destroyed Sheshharis oil terminal, Grushova refinery, shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS, and missile ship Novorossysk (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian cargo ship ZAID moved 35,000 t of stolen Ukrainian wheat from Sevastopol to Iskenderun, using AIS spoofing (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
Massive rocket barrage hit Kyiv, damaging over 40 sites, killing at least one and injuring 21 civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Multiple cruise missile alerts reported from Cherkasy, Cherkashchyna, and Chernihiv regions targeting Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/62620, @kpszsu/62606, @kpszsu/62607).
Ukrainian forces reported downed Shahed drones over Sumy region, causing casualties in a funeral convoy (Source: @UkraineNow/81401).
Russian UAVs spotted heading toward Vyshorod water reservoir and other locations in northern Kyiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/93469, @kpszsu/62638).
Ukrainian intelligence noted increased drone activity over southern Sumy and Romny corridors (Source: @eRadarrua/93447).

sections.executive_summary: A devastating drone strike on a Starobelsk college killed 21, marking the deadliest single attack on civilian infrastructure. Ukraine’s air‑defense and UAV units struck high‑value Russian logistics sites, crippling oil terminals and a shadow‑fleet tanker. Simultaneously, Russia continued illicit grain exports via the ship ZAID, while intense missile and drone barrages battered Kyiv and other front‑line areas.

points: Ukrainian UAVs successfully hit the Sheshharis oil terminal on the Black Sea, igniting fires and halting up to 75 Mt of oil throughput, a critical supply for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
A coordinated strike destroyed the Russian shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russia’s ability to transport seized grain and fuel (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian forces downed multiple Shahed drones over Sumy, including one that struck a funeral convoy, causing at least one severe injury (Source: @UkraineNow/81401).
Intelligence reports indicate increased Ukrainian drone sorties targeting Russian logistics hubs in occupied Crimea and the Black Sea corridor (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian air‑defense reported intercepts of cruise missiles launched from Cherkasy and Chernihiv regions, protecting Kyiv from further saturation attacks (Source: @kpszsu/62620, @kpszsu/62606).

The Russian cargo ship ZAID loaded over 35,000 t of Ukrainian wheat in Sevastopol, transited the Russian port of Caucasus for falsified documents, and proceeded to Iskenderun, Turkey, using AIS spoofing (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
Russian forces launched a massive combined rocket barrage on Kyiv, striking more than 40 locations, killing one civilian and injuring 21, including a 15‑year‑old (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Cruise missiles from the Cherkasy direction were launched toward Kyiv, with at least six missiles reported inbound (Source: @kpszsu/62620).
Russian UAVs were observed heading toward the Vyshorod water reservoir and other strategic points in northern Kyiv region, indicating intent to disrupt water supplies (Source: @eRadarrua/93469).
Continued use of Russian‑controlled airspace for illicit grain shipments, despite international sanctions, underscores Moscow’s reliance on shadow‑fleet logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
Cruise missiles launched from the east (Chernihiv region) were tracked heading toward Kyiv, with one missile reported over the Nizhyn district before descending (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Ukrainian forces reported downed Shahed drones over Sumy, with three drones striking a funeral convoy, highlighting ongoing Russian UAV pressure in the east (Source: @UkraineNow/81401).
Russian UAV groups were noted moving toward Romny and other locations in Sumy, suggesting preparation for further strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/93469).
Ukrainian air‑defense intercepted multiple cruise missiles from the east, reducing potential damage to eastern settlements (Source: @kpszsu/62626).
Reports of Russian UAVs over the Vyshorod water reservoir indicate attempts to target critical infrastructure in the eastern theater (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
Ukrainian radar detected cruise missiles launched from Chernihiv heading southwest toward Kyiv, with one missile over Nizhyn district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Multiple UAV clusters were observed over the Kyiv water reservoir, moving from the north toward the capital (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
Russian missile launches from the north of Poltava region were tracked heading south, indicating a broader northern strike pattern (Source: @kpszsu/62627).
Ukrainian forces reported intercepts of cruise missiles originating from the north‑west, protecting Kyiv’s northern suburbs (Source: @kpszsu/62607).
Continued alerts of missile activity from the north underscore heightened pressure on Kyiv’s air‑defense network (Source: @kpszsu/62606).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported heading southwest of Sumy toward Romny, indicating cross‑border strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/93469).
Cruise missiles launched from the south of Kyiv region were tracked heading toward the capital, part of a coordinated southern pressure campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62622).
Ukrainian forces reported downed Shahed drones over the southern Kyiv oblast, reducing Russian UAV threat in that sector (Source: @kpszsu/62631).
Russian UAV activity was noted over the southern Kyiv oblast, targeting infrastructure near Obukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/62630).
Continued missile alerts from the south suggest Russia is attempting to stretch Ukrainian air‑defense across multiple axes (Source: @kpszsu/62622).

Ukrainian air‑defense intercepted a wave of cruise missiles from Cherkasy, Chernihiv, and southern Kyiv regions, preventing larger civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/62620, @kpszsu/62606, @kpszsu/62622).
Russian UAVs conducted reconnaissance and strike missions over Kyiv’s water reservoirs and surrounding suburbs, indicating a focus on critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
Ukrainian forces successfully destroyed multiple Russian UAVs and Shahed drones over Sumy and Kyiv, demonstrating effective short‑range air‑defense (Source: @UkraineNow/81401).
Russian shadow‑fleet assets, including the tanker CHRYSALIS, were targeted and damaged by Ukrainian drones, reducing Russia’s maritime logistics capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Continued use of AIS spoofing by Russian vessels like ZAID highlights the maritime dimension of the air‑war, complicating detection and interception (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).

key_developments: A drone strike on Starobelsk college killed 21 people, marking the deadliest recent civilian attack (Source: @tass_agency/377250).
Ukrainian UAVs destroyed the Sheshharis oil terminal, Grushova refinery, and the shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS, crippling Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian cargo ship ZAID transported over 35,000 t of stolen Ukrainian wheat to Turkey, using AIS spoofing to evade detection (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
A massive combined rocket barrage hit Kyiv, damaging 40+ sites, killing one civilian and injuring 21 (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Multiple cruise missiles launched from Cherkasy, Chernihiv, and southern Kyiv regions were intercepted by Ukrainian air‑defense (Source: @kpszsu/62620, @kpszsu/62606, @kpszsu/62622).
Russian UAVs were observed heading toward the Vyshorod water reservoir, indicating attempts to target water infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/93469).
Ukrainian forces downed Shahed drones over Sumy, including one that struck a funeral convoy, causing civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81401).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying multi‑vector attacks, employing cruise missiles, UAV swarms, and illicit maritime logistics to sustain their war effort. Ukraine’s defensive successes against high‑value targets demonstrate growing UAV and air‑defense proficiency, yet civilian casualties in Kyiv reveal persistent vulnerability to saturation strikes. The continued export of stolen Ukrainian grain via shadow‑fleet vessels underscores Russia’s reliance on illicit economic channels to fund operations.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have condemned the grain theft and increased sanctions on Russia’s shadow‑fleet, while pledging additional UAV and air‑defense aid to Ukraine. Regional actors, notably Turkey, are under pressure to enforce maritime inspections to curb illicit grain shipments. NATO allies are monitoring the escalation of missile attacks on Kyiv, considering further air‑defense deployments.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of AIS spoofing and shadow‑fleet movements remains a priority to disrupt illicit grain logistics. Enhanced SIGINT on Russian missile launch sites in Cherkasy and Chernihiv is needed to pre‑empt future mass barrages. Continued UAV surveillance over Kyiv’s water infrastructure is essential to prevent sabotage of critical civilian services.
